target.name: Rain Industries
target.founder: Bryan Hatton, Ephraim Nowak, Maxwell Brodie

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rain Industries, a Seed-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Bryan Hatton, Ephraim Nowak, and Maxwell Brodie, has raised $14.7M to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector includes eight identified private peers such as Joby Aviation, Nuro, and Archer Aviation, though no public comparables exist in this space.

Given data constraints, Stage-Progression valuation is the applicable methodology. Using total raised ($14.7M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical 2.5x multiplier for progression from Seed to the next stage yields an implied valuation of $37M. This approach reflects observed step-ups in comparable financing rounds within the sector, though with limited granularity on Rain's specific metrics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $31M to $42M, centered on $37M. Key uncertainties include the absence of same-stage or adjacent-stage peer data—no private Seed-stage comparables in the Drones & Autonomous universe met the filtering threshold of three or more datapoints—which limits the precision of peer-median anchoring. Revenue multiples, customer traction, and technical differentiation remain unmeasured against the broader peer set. A more granular valuation would require either comparable fundraising rounds at Rain's exact stage or disclosed financial metrics from the identified peer universe.
